_comment: Multi-organ closed-loop coupling rules. All coupling writes go through FactorCommand via CouplingEngine.

couplings.name: RAAS → SVR (肾素-血管紧张素-醛固酮系统 → 体循环阻力)

references.text: Role of the renin-angiotensin system in the development of congestive heart failure in dogs. ACE inhibition (captopril) attenuated CO decrease and PAP increase in dog CHF models.

couplings.notes: Coefficient 0.20 (down from 0.30): canine RAAS contributes ~20-30% of SVR (Nelson Ch53) vs ~30-50% human (Guyton Ch26). At renin=2.0, SVR multiplier = 1.4 (+40%). Threshold 0.1 keeps RAAS dormant in healthy baseline. PMID:6320625 confirms ACE inhibition reduces SVR in dog CHF models.

couplings.name: RAAS → 心脏收缩力 (血管紧张素II直接心肌作用)

references.text: Positive chronotropic and inotropic effects of angiotensin II in the dog heart (1978). Isolated canine atria: positive chronotropic from 0.01 μg. Inotropic: inconsistent in atria, minimal in ventricles.
Chu et al. 2004. Differential inotropic effects of ET-1, Ang II, phenylephrine in dog ventricular myocardium. Ang II alone: no inotropic effect. Requires cAMP co-activation for small transient PIE.

couplings.notes: Coefficient 0.02 (down from 0.15): PMID:679973 shows Ang II has 'slight and inconsistent' inotropic effect in dog atria. PMID:jphs96-2-199 confirms Ang II alone has NO inotropic effect in dog ventricular muscle — only small transient PIE with cAMP elevation. At AngII=1.5, contractility multiplier = 1.03 (+3%).

couplings.name: 醛固酮 → 血管容积 (钠水潴留)

references.text: Experimental aldosterone hypertension in the dog (n=8 conscious dogs). Chronic aldosterone infusion 14 μg/kg/day: BV +10.5% (64→70.7 mL/kg Day 4), ECFV +10.7% (318→352 mL/kg Day 3). MAP stabilized at 132%.

couplings.notes: Disabled pending a retained-volume-rate design. The literature anchor is chronic (days), while current FactorCommand multiply semantics compound every step on a stateful volume variable. Target was moved to heart.blood_volume because heart.circulating_volume_ml is the engine's authoritative circulating-volume state; simply writing fluid.vascular_volume_ml would not drive hemodynamics cleanly. PMID:7040227 remains the future anchor once a rate/integral pathway exists.

couplings.name: GFR → 血尿素氮 (肾排泄功能下降)

references.text: Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 53 — BUN rises proportionally as GFR declines below 50% of normal. BUN/Cr ratio helps distinguish pre-renal from renal azotemia.
Restuito et al. 2018. Reference intervals for serum urea nitrogen and creatinine in client-owned dogs. Vet Clin Pathol.

couplings.notes: BUN = 20 + 40×(1 - GFR/100): at GFR=100 (normal), BUN=20; at GFR=50, BUN=40; at GFR=20, BUN=52. Condition gfr<90 captures mild-moderate renal dysfunction. time_constant=0 (immediate algebraic mapping).

couplings.name: MAP → GFR (肾自主调节，犬科特异性)

references.text: Autoregulation of renal blood flow, GFR and renin release in conscious dogs (n=22 foxhounds). GFR perfectly autoregulated 160-81 mmHg. Break-off: 80.5±3.5 mmHg. GFR ceases at 40 mmHg. Renin threshold: 95 mmHg.

couplings.notes: COMPLETE REWRITE based on canine experimental data. Piecewise function: MAP>=81 → GFR multiplier=1.0 (perfect autoregulation). MAP 81-40 → linear decline to 0. MAP<40 → GFR=0. Source: PMID:3324052, 22 chronically instrumented conscious foxhounds. Break-off point 80.5±3.5 mmHg. Previous linear formula (0.004 coefficient) was from human data and incorrect for dogs.

couplings.name: 动脉氧分压 → 心肌收缩力 (心肌氧供)

references.text: Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 22 — Myocardial oxygen delivery (CaO2 × coronary blood flow) directly determines contractility. Hypoxemia reduces ATP production and contractility.

couplings.notes: Coefficient 0.006: at PaO2=60 mmHg (moderate hypoxemia), contractility multiplier = min(1+0.006×(-35), 1.3) = 0.79 (-21%). Disabled — requires validation against patient data before enabling.

couplings.name: 动脉二氧化碳分压 → 呼吸驱动 (化学感受器)

references.text: Response time and sensitivity of ventilatory response to CO2 in unanesthetized intact dogs. Central chemoreceptors contribute ~63% of steady-state CO2 ventilatory gain. Wide dog-to-dog variability.

couplings.notes: Coefficient 0.035 (down from 0.05): PMID:16166236 — canine ventilatory CO2 response: central chemoreceptors contribute ~63% gain, peripheral ~37%. Dog-to-dog variability is wide. At PaCO2=60, multiplier = 1+0.035×20 = 1.70 (+70%). Nonlinear interaction between central and peripheral chemoreceptors (PMC2915520) means linear model is approximate.

couplings.name: 肺扩散系数 → 动脉氧分压

references.text: Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 40 — Fick's law of diffusion: O2 transfer across alveolar membrane proportional to diffusion coefficient (D = VA×DLO2 / thickness). Impaired diffusion reduces PaO2.

couplings.notes: PaO2 scaled by diffusion_coefficient: at DC=0.5 (impaired diffusion), PaO2 reduced 50%. Disabled — direct DC→PaO2 algebraic mapping oversimplifies ventilation-perfusion effects. Needs pulmonary blood flow integration.

couplings.name: 肾GFR → 代谢性酸中毒 (酸性物质蓄积)

references.text: Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 30 — Metabolic acidosis in renal failure due to impaired H+ secretion, decreased NH4+ production, and retention of organic acids.
Kraut & Madias, 2000. Metabolic acidosis in chronic kidney disease: pathophysiology and consequences. Kidney Int.

couplings.notes: pH formula: at GFR=80 (normal), pH=7.4; at GFR=40 (moderate CKD), pH=7.4-0.4×0.5=7.2 (metabolic acidosis). Disabled — simplistic algebraic mapping ignores joint contribution of respiratory compensation. Needs HCO3 integration.

couplings.name: 动脉PCO2 → 碳酸氢根 (呼吸代偿)

references.text: Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 30 — Primary respiratory acidosis stimulates renal compensation: for each 10 mmHg rise in PaCO2, HCO3 rises ~4 mEq/L acutely and ~4-5 mEq/L over 2-5 days.

couplings.notes: HCO3 formula: at PaCO2=60 (hypercapnia), HCO3 = 24 - 0.3×20 = 18 mEq/L (appropriate respiratory compensation). Disabled — needs coordination with blood buffer pair (HCO3/CO2) in acid-base module.

couplings.name: 乳酸 → pH偏移 (乳酸性酸中毒)

references.text: Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 30 — Lactic acidosis: accumulation of lactate from tissue hypoperfusion or anaerobic glycolysis generates H+, lowering arterial pH.

couplings.notes: pH offset: at lactate=10 mM (severe sepsis), pH drops -0.05×9 = -0.45 (pH 6.95, severe acidemia). Condition lactate>2.0 ensures no effect in normal physiological range. Disabled — lactate clearance by liver not modeled, effect may be overstated.

couplings.name: 代谢性酸中毒 → 血钾转移 (酸碱-电解质交互)

references.text: Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 27 — Acidosis causes extracellular shift of K+ as H+ enters cells in exchange for K+. For each 0.1 unit decrease in pH, serum K+ rises ~0.3-0.7 mEq/L.

couplings.notes: K+ shift: at pH=7.0 (severe acidosis), K+ rises -0.3×0.4 = +1.2 mEq/L above baseline. Coefficient -0.3 matches 0.3 mEq/L per 0.1 pH unit. Condition pH<7.35 activates in acidemia. Disabled — only captures acidosis effect, not total K+ balance.

couplings.name: 肝代谢 → 纤维蛋白原合成 (凝血因子)

references.text: Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 37 — Hepatocellular disease reduces synthesis of clotting factors (I, II, V, VII, IX, X, XI) and fibrinogen, prolonging PT and PTT.
Stokol et al. 2000. Evaluation of coagulation in dogs with liver disease. J Vet Intern Med.

couplings.notes: Fibrinogen scaled by liver metabolic_activity: at MA=0.5 (half liver function), fibrinogen = 50% of normal. Disabled — can cause fibrinogen <50 mg/dL in mild hepatic dysfunction where full compensation should be expected.

couplings.name: 肝代谢 → 白蛋白合成 (血浆蛋白)

references.text: Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 37 — Albumin synthesized exclusively by hepatocytes; serum albumin declines with reduced liver synthetic function.

couplings.notes: Albumin scaled by liver metabolic_activity: at MA=0.5, albumin = 50% of normal (hypoalbuminemia). Half-life ~8 days in dogs — too slow for acute simulation. Disabled for baseline stability.

couplings.name: 肝代谢 → PT延长 (凝血酶原时间)

references.text: Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 37 — PT (extrinsic pathway) prolonged in hepatocellular disease due to decreased Factor VII synthesis (shortest half-life ~6h).

couplings.notes: PT formula: at MA=0.3 (severe liver dysfunction), PT increases +5×0.7 = +3.5 sec (moderate prolongation). Baseline PT 10-15 sec → 13.5 sec in liver failure. Disabled — Factor VII kinetics not modeled, static PT increase is crude approximation.

couplings.name: 肝代谢 → ALT释放 (细胞损伤)

references.text: Nelson & Couto, Small Animal Internal Medicine, 5th ed. Chapter 37 — ALT (alanine aminotransferase) released from damaged hepatocytes; marked elevation indicates acute hepatocellular injury.

couplings.notes: ALT formula: at MA=0.2 (severe liver failure), ALT rises +200×0.8 = +160 U/L. Normal canine ALT 10-100 U/L → 260 U/L (markedly elevated). Disabled — ALT elevation due to leakage, not reduced synthesis; opposite direction from other liver coag rules.

couplings.name: 中心静脉压 → 肾灌注 (静脉充血对GFR的影响)

references.text: Guyton & Hall, Textbook of Medical Physiology, 14th ed. Chapter 26 — Elevated right atrial pressure (backward congestion) reduces renal perfusion gradient and GFR via Starling mechanisms and increased renal interstitial pressure.
Fadem et al. 1982. Effect of elevated central venous pressure on renal function in dogs. Am J Physiol.

couplings.notes: CVP→GFR formula: at CVP=15 mmHg (severe right heart failure), multiplier = max(0.3, 1-0.02×10) = 0.8 (20% reduction). At CVP=8 (threshold), multiplier = 1.0 (no effect). time_constant=10s reflects venous congestion dynamics. Disabled — CVP calibration against real hemodynamic data needed.
